Class ArrayPool

java.lang.Object
com.github.mrgarbagegamer.ArrayPool
public final class ArrayPool extends Object
A high-performance, non-thread-safe object pool for recycling fixed-size short[] arrays.

This pool is a critical component of the memory management strategy for the CombinationGeneratorTask. Its primary purpose is to eliminate memory allocation on the hot path by pre-allocating and reusing prefix arrays used during generation. The fundamental rule of high-performance JVM tuning is to avoid allocations, and this class is a key tool for adhering to that principle.

Design and Implementation

The pool is implemented as a simple circular buffer using a 2D array, which provides O(1) get() and put(short[]) operations with minimal overhead. This design was chosen for its simplicity and raw performance over more complex, and potentially higher-overhead, lock-free data structures.

Usage and Thread Safety

This class is not thread-safe and is designed to be used exclusively within a ThreadLocal context. Each generator must own a dedicated instance of ArrayPool to prevent race conditions.

Since:
2025.07 - Custom Generator Pools
See Also:
  • CombinationGeneratorTask
  • TaskPool
Performance Characteristics:
O(1) for both get() and put(short[]) operations. The constructor is O(capacity) due to pre-allocation.
Threading Model:
This class is explicitly not thread-safe. All access must be confined to a single thread.
Memory Management:
Pre-allocated at construction to avoid runtime allocation, with a memory footprint determined by the capacity and numClicks fields.

  • Field Details

    • numClicks

      private static int numClicks
      The required size for all arrays managed by the pool. This value is shared across all ArrayPool instances.

      This field must be set via setNumClicks(int) before any ArrayPool is instantiated. It is static to ensure that all generated combination arrays are of a consistent, predictable size, which is essential for the pre-allocation strategy. Making it non-final (but settable only once) was a design trade-off to avoid dependencies on configuration files or system properties for initialization.

      Since:
      2025.07 - ArrayPool Pre-allocation
      See Also:
      • ArrayPool(int)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; effectively immutable after being set once.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes as a primitive int.

    • arrays

      private final short[][] arrays
      The internal buffer storing the pre-allocated short[] arrays.

      This 2D array acts as the backing store for the circular buffer. It is allocated once at construction with a fixed capacity, and its arrays are recycled via the head and tail pointers.

      Since:
      2025.07 - Custom Generator Pools
      See Also:
      • capacity
      • numClicks
      • ArrayPool(int)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access for get/put operations.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; must be confined to a single thread.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of ~capacity * numClicks * 2 bytes as a short[][].

    • capacity

      private final int capacity
      The maximum number of arrays the pool can hold.

      The capacity is set at construction and determines the buffer's memory footprint. A larger capacity reduces the chance of the pool running out of arrays (which would return null), but increases initial memory usage. The optimal size depends on the workload and the expected depth of the recursive tasks.

      Since:
      2025.07 - Custom Generator Pools
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) initialization and access.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; immutable after construction.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes as a primitive int.

    • head

      private int head
      The index of the next available array to be retrieved by get().

      This pointer advances through the circular buffer as arrays are dispensed. Using separate head and tail pointers was chosen over a single-pointer-with-size design to remove arithmetic from the hot path of get/put operations.

      Since:
      2025.07 - Custom Generator Pools
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access and update.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; must be confined to a single thread.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes as a primitive int.

    • tail

      private int tail
      The index where the next recycled array will be placed by put(short[]).

      This pointer advances through the circular buffer as arrays are returned. Using separate head and tail pointers was chosen over a single-pointer-with-size design to remove arithmetic from the hot path of get()/put operations.

      Since:
      2025.07 - Custom Generator Pools
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access and update.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; must be confined to a single thread.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes as a primitive int.

    • size

      private int size
      The current number of arrays available in the pool.

      This value is decremented by get() and incremented by put(short[]). It is used to check if the pool is empty or full. While the size could be calculated from the head and tail pointers, storing it explicitly makes the checks trivial and avoids arithmetic in the hot path.

      Since:
      2025.07 - Custom Generator Pools
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access and update.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; must be confined to a single thread.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes as a primitive int.

  • Constructor Details

    • ArrayPool

      public ArrayPool(int capacity)
      Constructs a new ArrayPool and pre-allocates its internal array buffer.

      All arrays are allocated upfront to prevent runtime allocation overhead. This constructor will fail if numClicks has not been set, ensuring a fail-fast approach to configuration errors.

      Parameters:
      capacity - The maximum number of arrays the pool can hold. Must be positive.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if capacity is not positive or if numClicks has not been set.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Custom Generator Pools
      See Also:
      • arrays
      • setNumClicks(int)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(capacity) pre-allocation of arrays.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a short[capacity][numClicks] and an ArrayPool instance.

  • Method Details

    • setNumClicks

      public static void setNumClicks(int numClicks)
      Sets the static size for all arrays that will be managed by ArrayPool instances.

      This method is not thread-safe and must be called exactly once during application initialization, before any threads begin creating or using pools.

      Parameters:
      numClicks - The size of each array in the pool. Must be positive.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if numClicks is not positive.
      Since:
      2025.07 - ArrayPool Pre-allocation
      See Also:
      • numClicks
      • ArrayPool(int)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) assignment.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; must be called during single-threaded initialization.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • get

      public short[] get()
      Retrieves an array from the pool.

      This is an O(1) operation. To improve performance on the hot path, this method assumes the caller will handle a null return gracefully. The slot previously holding the array is nulled out to assist the garbage collector, a micro-optimization that can be beneficial in long-running, high-allocation scenarios.

      Returns:
      A pre-allocated short[] array, or null if the pool is empty.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Custom Generator Pools
      See Also:
      • isEmpty()
      • put(short[])
      • size()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) retrieval and field updates.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • put

      public void put(short[] array)
      Returns an array to the pool, making it available for reuse.

      This is an O(1) operation. If the pool is already at full capacity, this method returns immediately and the given array is effectively dropped. This can happen if arrays are generated faster than they are consumed, but a properly sized pool should prevent this.

      Parameters:
      array - The short[] array to return to the pool. It is assumed to be non-null and of the correct size.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Custom Generator Pools
      See Also:
      • ArrayPool(int)
      • get()
      • setNumClicks(int)
      • size()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) insertion and field updates.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Checks if the pool contains no available arrays.
      Returns:
      true if the pool is empty, false otherwise.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Custom Generator Pools
      See Also:
      • get()
      • size()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) retrieval and comparison.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • size

      public int size()
      Returns the number of arrays currently available in the pool.
      Returns:
      The current number of arrays in the pool.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Custom Generator Pools
      See Also:
      • get()
      • isEmpty()
      • put(short[])
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) retrieval.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

Performance-optimized brute-force solver for hexagonal grid Lights Out puzzles
